reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome real download+run on WASM via CDP, dtype sweep across q4/q8/fp16/fp32, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser generation (headless Chrome, WASM/CPU, transformers.js 4.2.0, dtype fp32): prompt 'Name three primary colors.' → 'Three primary colors are red, yellow, and blue.' — coherent, correct, NON-degenerate, 0 console errors. The built basics page was then driven end-to-end (click Download → ready → typed a prompt → streamed an assistant reply). DTYPE SWEEP disclosed: q4 and q8 both abort at ORT-Web session creation on the WASM EP ('Could not find an implementation for GatherBlockQuantized(1) node /model/embed_tokens/Gather_Quant'); fp16/q4f16 need WebGPU's fp16 kernels; fp32 (onnx/model.onnx) is the ONLY export whose ops all run on the WASM EP. So the worker uses fp32 on WASM and q4f16 on WebGPU.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real coherent WASM output; the quantized-export limitation is disclosed on the page (at-a-glance 'Why fp32 on WASM?').

rubric.evidence: text-generation causal LM: messages array → chat template → streamed tokens. Output is fluent, on-topic natural language for simple prompts (verified). The 'see inside' panel reads real last-position logits [1,seq,vocab] over the ~262k Gemma vocabulary, softmaxes, and shows a genuine top-k distribution.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/gemma-3-270m-it-ONNX, linked), task (text-generation), architecture (gemma3_text), dtype per backend (q4f16 WebGPU / fp32 WASM), download sizes (~200 MB / ~1.05 GB), and the pinned runtime (Transformers.js 4.2.0, scoped LOCALLY to this worker only — the gemma3_text class is newer than the shared 3.7.5; precedent sam2/ernie). Inference runs in a module Web Worker.
rubric.notes: Version-pin escape hatch verified scoped to models/gemma-3-270m/worker.js; shared lib/webai.js stays 3.7.5.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). Headless fresh profile showed 'Download model (~1050 MB)'; after the click it loaded to state=ready and generated, exposing a 'Clear cached model' control. requiresWebGPU:false — runs on the universal WASM/CPU path and upgrades to WebGPU (q4f16) when a real adapter is present. Never silently re-downloads.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified download→ready→generate; honest large-download disclosure up front.

rubric.evidence: A role=status aria-live #status region reports 'Generating on the <backend>…' / 'Done.' / 'Generation failed: …'; a Stop button interrupts generation (InterruptableStoppingCriteria); the shared loader streams download progress and offers Retry. Failures surface on the page, not only the console.
rubric.notes: Stop, Clear-conversation, and Retry all present.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled, keyboard-operable controls: message textarea (Enter to send), Send, Stop, system-prompt textarea, temperature/top_p sliders with live value readouts, max-new-tokens number, a greedy checkbox that disables the sampling sliders, example-prompt chips, and Clear-conversation. Use-case pages add persona chips (wild), sample chips + JSON validation (practical), and a two-model RAG (multi-model).

Every token, the latency/tok-s readout, and the top-k distribution come from the real on-device run (verified). No canned text. On WASM-only devices the fp32 path runs for real rather than faking a WebGPU result; if a run fails the error is shown, never a placebo reply.

Buttons expose accessible names; the message + system inputs have associated labels (visually-hidden label on the composer); state announced via role=status aria-live; persona chips use aria-pressed. Streaming caret animation respects prefers-reduced-motion. No images (text only).

responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) + mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean (0px horizontal overflow, light+dark). The at-a-glance table (table-layout:fixed, overflow-wrap:anywhere), chat transcript, composer (flex-wrap), and sampling controls collapse cleanly on mobile; 44px min tap targets on Send/Stop/chips.
rubric.notes: Agent-reviewed both device classes via responsive-check.

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in a module Web Worker with TextStreamer streaming tokens back via postMessage; the main thread only appends text, so token streaming stays INP-safe and never blocks input. modern-web-guidance identify-inp-causes + break-up-long-tasks consulted and applied.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy JS off the interaction path so INP stays low; stream incrementally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation runs in a Web Worker; tokens stream via postMessage and the main thread only appends text nodes, so the compose/send interaction stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: models/gemma-3-270m/worker.js (TextStreamer + postMessage); gemma.js appends tokens; 0 console errors in the browser run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model load + inference is off the main thread in a module worker.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Let critical work (user input) run ahead of background work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference is fully off-thread so the input textarea and Stop button stay immediately responsive during streaming.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid, wrapping layout; avoid fixed widths; fixed-layout table with wrapping cells to avoid horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .glance uses table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere; composer/controls flex-wrap; responsive-check desktop+mobile 0px overflow.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; respect reduced motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #status, labelled inputs, aria-pressed persona chips, prefers-reduced-motion on the caret.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: index.html + wild/index.html markup; GEMMA_CSS caret media query

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css.

openQuestions.question: On WASM the only runnable export is fp32 (~1.05 GB) because the quantized builds use the WebGPU-only GatherBlockQuantized op. Worth watching upstream for a WASM-compatible quantized re-export to shrink the CPU-path download.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a fine-tuning explainer/demo showing how a 270M base like this is cheap to specialise for one narrow task (classification/extraction) and embed in a page.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for gemma-3-270m (onnx-community/gemma-3-270m-it-ONNX, Google's smallest Gemma 3, instruction-tuned, gemma3_text). Real coherent NON-degenerate generation verified in headless Chrome on the WASM/CPU path (dtype fp32): 'Name three primary colors.' → 'Three primary colors are red, yellow, and blue.', 0 console errors. Full dtype sweep disclosed: q4/q8 abort on the WASM EP (GatherBlockQuantized is WebGPU-only), fp16/q4f16 need WebGPU — so the worker runs fp32 on WASM and q4f16 on WebGPU, stated on the page. Pins Transformers.js 4.2.0 LOCALLY in this worker (gemma3_text newer than the shared 3.7.5). Streaming chat + real top-k distribution, sampling controls, persona/extraction/RAG use cases. Desktop+mobile automated-clean. Distinct from the WebGPU-only Gemma 3 1B WebLLM demo (this is the Transformers.js WASM/WebGPU build of the smallest Gemma 3, runs with no GPU).
